subject: [RFC PATCH v2 0/7] mm, swap: Virtual Swap Space (Swap Table Edition)

reviews.author: Nhat Pham (author)
reviews.summary: Author addressed reviewer Yosry Ahmed's concern about code divergence and suggested abstraction of xarray operations vs vswap operations into helpers, specifically zswap_entry_load() and zswap_entry_store(). The author agreed to restructure the code in a future version (v2) to use these helpers and reduce duplication.

reviews.author: Yosry Ahmed
reviews.summary: Reviewer Yosry Ahmed suggested abstracting xarray operations vs vswap operations into helpers, specifically zswap_entry_load() and zswap_entry_store(), to improve code readability and reduce divergence.

2026-06-24.patch_summary: This patch series introduces Virtual Swap Space (Swap Table Edition), which aims to address the limitations of current swap design by decoupling swap entries from physical backing slots. This allows for more efficient use of disk space, especially in scenarios where zswap is used. The changes include rebasing to a newer mm-unstable tip, fixing assorted issues, and simplifying code. Additionally, the patch series introduces new features such as deferring memcg_table allocation on physical clusters until the first vswap-backing slot installs and widening swap_info_struct->max and ->pages to support up to 8 PB of swap space.
